name: tech-reader
displayName: Tech Reader — Read Code Aloud, Explained
description: Listen to code, comments, and design specs read aloud in plain English. A local Ollama model explains what code does (no verbatim variable names, no "underscore"), and your OS voices speak it — fully offline, no API keys, nothing leaves your machine.

enumDescriptions: Web Speech API with your OS voices, played directly in the reader.
macOS `say` rendered on the host and played as audio in the reader.
Piper neural TTS rendered on the host — natural local voices (requires the piper CLI and a voice model).
techReader.audioEngine.markdownDescription: How speech audio is produced. `piper` gives the most natural local voices (install the `piper` CLI + a voice model); `webspeech`/`say` use the OS voices. Reload the reader after changing.

techReader.hostPlayback.markdownDescription: Play audio on the host with short-lived `afplay` processes (like the `say` command) instead of a persistent `<audio>` element in the reader. Use this if VS Code's audio wedges your system sound (e.g. with a flaky USB audio device). Applies to the `say`/`piper` engines. Reload the reader after changing.

enumDescriptions: Explain code and read prose naturally using the local Ollama model.
Read everything with the offline humanizer — no AI, instant.
techReader.mode.description: How content is turned into speech. You can also toggle this from the reader toolbar.

enumDescriptions: Let the AI smooth prose for natural listening (keeps all detail).
Read prose faithfully through the offline humanizer.
techReader.proseHandling.description: In AI mode, how to handle prose (Markdown / comments / design specs).

enumDescriptions: Explain what the code does in plain English.
Read the code with identifiers humanized, no AI.
Distill the table's key takeaway in a sentence or two (AI mode).

techReader.ollama.baseUrl.description: URL of your local Ollama server.

techReader.ollama.model.markdownDescription: Ollama model used for explanations. Pull one first, e.g. `ollama pull llama3.2` or `ollama pull qwen2.5-coder`.

techReader.ollama.temperature.description: Sampling temperature for the model. Lower is more focused.

techReader.ollama.keepAlive.description: How long Ollama keeps the model loaded in memory between calls.

techReader.ollama.idleTimeoutMs.description: Give up on an Ollama stream that produces no output for this many milliseconds, falling back to the offline humanizer.

techReader.voiceURI.description: Preferred Web Speech voice (set from the reader's voice picker).

techReader.sayVoice.description: Preferred macOS `say` voice (set from the reader's voice picker when using the `say` engine).

techReader.piper.binPath.markdownDescription: Path to the `piper` executable. Leave empty to auto-detect (`~/.local/bin/piper`, Homebrew, `/usr/local/bin`). Install with `uv tool install piper-tts`.

techReader.piper.voicesDir.markdownDescription: Directory of Piper `.onnx` voice models. Each `.onnx` appears as a voice in the reader's picker. Download voices from https://huggingface.co/rhasspy/piper-voices.

techReader.piper.model.markdownDescription: Path to the active Piper `.onnx` model (set from the reader's voice picker). Empty = first model found in the voices directory.

techReader.piper.lengthScale.description: Piper pacing (phoneme length). 1 = normal, higher = slower. Playback speed is also adjustable live in the reader.

techReader.locale.description: Language used for sentence segmentation (BCP-47 base, e.g. "en", "de").

techReader.highlightWhileReading.description: Highlight and auto-scroll to the sentence being spoken.

techReader.dictionary.markdownDescription: Extra abbreviation expansions for the offline humanizer, e.g. `{ "k8s": "kubernetes", "repo": "repository" }`.
